scenarios.name: Specific Performance of Sale Agreement

checks.detail: Specific Relief Act / specific performance cited
At least one statutory section cited
Prayer for execution of sale deed
No invented amounts (10L/50L never in facts)

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE District Court / Civil Court
AT Bengaluru

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Anita Sharma,
Aged about 52 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Indiranagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Rajesh Reddy,
Aged about 60 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: HSR Layout, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE OF CONTRACT WITH COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the suit. The Defendant is a person sui juris and can be sued. The suit property is situated within Bengaluru and the Defendant resides within Bengaluru, hence this Hon'ble Court has jurisdiction under Section 16 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908. The suit value is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Court, and this Court is competent to try the suit under Section 9 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

LIMITATION

2. The suit is within limitation under Article 54 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, the period prescribed being three years from the date on which the contract was broken. The suit is filed well within the prescribed period.

FACTS OF THE AGREEMENT

3. On 10.01.2024, the Plaintiff and Defendant entered into an Agreement to Sell regarding Flat No. 302, 3rd Floor, Sunshine Apartments, Koramangala, Bengaluru, measuring 1200 sq ft, for a total consideration of Rs. 85,00,000/-. The terms of the agreement are recorded in the document marked as Annexure A.
4. On the date of the agreement, i.e., 10.01.2024, the Plaintiff paid an advance amount of Rs. 25,00,000/- to the Defendant via RTGS bearing UTR No. HDFC20240110987654. The proof of payment is marked as Annexure B.
5. The remaining balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/- was agreed to be paid on or before 10.07.2024 against the execution and registration of the Sale Deed.

PLAINTIFF'S READINESS AND WILLINGNESS

6. The Plaintiff has always been and continues to be ready and willing to perform her part of the contract. The Plaintiff has arranged the balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/- and is ready to pay the same upon execution of the Sale Deed.
7. The Plaintiff has consistently communicated her willingness to proceed with the transaction and has never defaulted on any obligation under the agreement.

DEFENDANT'S REFUSAL TO PERFORM

8. On 05.06.2024, the Defendant sent a letter refusing to execute the Sale Deed, claiming he had received a higher offer for the suit property. A copy of the refusal letter is marked as Annexure C.
9. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 20.06.2024 calling upon the Defendant to execute the Sale Deed as per the agreement. A copy of the legal notice with postal receipt is marked as Annexure D. The Defendant refused to comply with the notice.

LEGAL BASIS

10. The Plaintiff is entitled to specific performance under Section 10 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963, as the contract is in writing and duly signed, and the Court shall grant specific performance subject to exceptions under the Act.
11. The Plaintiff satisfies the requirements of Section 16 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963, as she has continuously performed or ready to perform her part of the contract, evidenced by the payment of advance and arrangement of balance funds.
12. The contract is valid and enforceable under Section 10 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, as it is made by competent parties, for lawful consideration, and with free consent.
13. This Court has the authority to try the suit under Section 9 and Section 16 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, and to award interest under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.
14. The Plaintiff seeks relief under Section 21 and Section 22 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963 for compensation and possession if necessary. The principles of Part Performance under Section 53A of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882 are applicable to protect the Plaintiff's interest in the suit property.
15. The contract is not determinable nor involves personal service, thus no bar under the Specific Relief Act applies. Time is not of the essence in this contract for immovable property unless expressly stipulated.

CAUSE OF ACTION

16. The cause of action first arose on 05.06.2024 when the Defendant expressly refused to execute the Sale Deed despite the Plaintiff's readiness and willingness to perform.
17. The cause of action continues to subsist as the Defendant has failed to perform his obligations under the agreement. The suit is filed within the limitation period prescribed under Article 54 of the Limitation Act, 1963.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

18. For the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee, the suit is valued at Rs. 85,00,000/- (Rupees Eighty Five Lakhs Only), being the total consideration agreed upon in the Agreement to Sell.
19. The requisite court fee of {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} has been paid on the plaint under the relevant provisions of the Court Fees Act. The plaint is properly stamped.

PRAYER

20. In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:
(a) Pass a decree for specific performance of the Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024 and direct the Defendant to execute and register the Sale Deed in favour of the Plaintiff upon payment of the balance consideration;
(b) Direct the Defendant to deliver vacant and peaceful possession of the suit property to the Plaintiff if not already in possession;
(c) In the alternative, award compensation under Section 21 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963 in lieu of or in addition to specific performance, at such amount as this Hon'ble Court deems fit;
(d) Award pendente lite and future interest on the amount paid under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 respectively;
(e) Award costs of the suit to the Plaintiff;
(f) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

SCHEDULE OF SUIT PROPERTY

Flat No. 302, 3rd Floor, Sunshine Apartments, Koramangala, Bengaluru, measuring 1200 sq ft.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Annexure A — Original agreement dated 10.01.2024

Annexure B — RTGS receipt for Rs. 25,00,000 (UTR: HDFC20240110987654)

Annexure C — Defendant's refusal letter dated 05.06.2024

Annexure D — Legal notice dated 20.06.2024 with postal receipt

VERIFICATION

Verified at Bengaluru on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

I, Anita Sharma, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 20 of this plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and no material facts have been concealed therefrom, and that I have not filed any other suit, petition, or proceeding in any court or before any authority in respect of the same subject matter and cause of action.

DEPONENT

Anita Sharma

PLAINTIFF

                                    THROUGH:
                                    {{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
                                    Advocate for the Plaintiff
                                    Enrollment No. {{ENROLLMENT_NO}}
                                    {{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}
                                    Mobile: {{ADVOCATE_PHONE}}
                                    Email: {{ADVOCATE_EMAIL}}

checks.detail: Total claim Rs.32L or breakdown stated

CONSISTENCY GATE — FORBIDDEN CONTENT

The civil drafting pipeline stopped before finalization for the following reasons:
1. UNANCHORED_THEORY: Draft pleads readiness and willingness in a pure damages contract suit.

Clarify the above issues and rerun drafting.
